story_summary: Keith Cobb was appointed to lead KPMG's newly reorganized Financial Services unit, faced partner skepticism due to his non-specialist background, mounted a highly prepared, consultant-backed presentation at a national meeting to establish credibility and drive adoption of a five-point strategy, and later accepted an external CEO offer from Alamo.

context.role: Leader, KPMG Financial Services business unit (national managing partner)
context.stakes: Firm-wide reorganization success, partner buy-in, client service quality, financial performance, and personal career decision

core_conflict: Keith Cobb lacked a traditional specialized financial-services pedigree yet had to establish credibility and lead over four thousand specialists after a disruptive reorganization, while also later deciding whether to leave KPMG for an external CEO role.

decision_moments.trigger: Widespread partner grumbling and explicit questions about his selection and credentials shortly after the reorganization.
constraints: He was perceived as not a recognized financial services expert
Deep-seated provincial incentives and geographic silos in firm culture
Large audience of skeptical, experienced partners (≈400 at meeting)
Need for rapid resolution to lead 4,000 professionals effectively
options: Do nothing and hope skepticism dissipates
Address concerns privately through one-on-one meetings
Mount a public, high-visibility event to present a clear strategy and establish leadership
decision_moments.choice: Organize and execute a high-visibility Orlando meeting, develop a clear five-point strategy, and prepare the presentation intensively with outside experts (Marc Gerstein, Myles Martel, Kathleen Hessert).
decision_moments.reasoning: He concluded the rumbling had to be dispelled quickly to lead the group and that a decisive, well-delivered public presentation could change partner sentiment and drive the new strategy.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Personal reputational exposure if the presentation failed; significant investment of time and resources in consultants and preparation; potential to inflame critics if mishandled.
decision_moments.outcome: The presentation was received as an unequivocal success; critics were silenced, partner pushback "stopped in its tracks," and the strategy began to unfold nationally.

decision_moments.trigger: Phone call from Mike Egan offering the role of vice chairman and chief executive officer of Alamo Rent A Car (February 1995).
constraints: Thirty-two-year career and important role at KPMG
Informal offer with loose ends and open details
Need to consult KPMG chairman and consider firm commitments
options: Decline the offer and remain at KPMG
Negotiate terms while remaining at KPMG
Consult with KPMG leadership then accept the external role
decision_moments.choice: Consulted with KPMG chairman Jon Madonna and then accepted the Alamo offer, signed a formal contract, and tendered resignation from KPMG.
decision_moments.reasoning: Jon Madonna advised it was an extraordinary opportunity that few KPMG partners would see, supporting acceptance; Cobb viewed it as a unique career chance.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Leaving a long-term, influential position at KPMG and embracing uncertainty of a turnaround CEO role outside the firm.
decision_moments.outcome: He signed a formal contract with Alamo and resigned from KPMG after a 32-year career.

principles.principle: Make superior client service the organizing priority.
principles.evidence: Understand that superior client service is everything,

principles.principle: Treat risk management as a central, firm-wide service theme.
principles.evidence: Identify risk management as a central service theme,

principles.principle: Create systems and incentives to overcome provincialism and enable firm-wide expertise sharing.
principles.evidence: Our strategy stripped out the provincial incentives and provided motivation for partners to reach out beyond their immediate environments for specialized expertise.

principles.principle: Use exhaustive preparation and effective communications to alter organizational momentum.
principles.evidence: This one event became a case study for me about how exhaustive preparation and effective communications can positively alter the course of a business.

principles.principle: Build internal knowledge management to harness firm resources.
principles.evidence: Build an internal knowledge management system to harness resources and skills, with all professionals capable of accessing the database.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: When credibility is contested, stage a high-visibility, strategy-focused event to reset the narrative.
decision_heuristics.evidence: This meeting was to be the turning point in the transformation of the financial services practice.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Recruit domain and communication experts to shape strategy and presentation rather than relying solely on internal judgment.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I was fortunate to meet Marc Gerstein, who would prove tremendously helpful in the design of an overarching strategy for our newly reorganized practice.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Rehearse presentations intensively with videotaped critique until delivery is flawless.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I practiced my entire presentation a dozen or more times, with her videotaping it and following up with harsh criticism and detailed recommendations for improvement.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Align incentives and allocate top talent to priority clients regardless of geography.
decision_heuristics.evidence: Allocate KPMG talent effectively and efficiently, with the best talent assigned to the most important clients and opportunities, wherever they might be located.

leadership_patterns.risk_tolerance: Willing to accept reputational and career risk to drive large-scale structural and cultural change
leadership_patterns.communication_style: Deliberate, polished, high-visibility, and rehearsed; leverages expert coaches and crafted messaging
leadership_patterns.accountability_posture: Takes ownership for firm-wide implementation and personally drives execution and follow-through
leadership_patterns.ethical_framing: Client-centered and risk-aware, emphasizing appropriate expertise deployment to protect clients and the firm

quotes.quote: Who is Keith Cobb and how on earth did he get chosen for this role?
I had to dispel this rumbling if I was to lead the group of four thousand professionals.
So, I threw my entire energy into the Orlando meeting.
